title: Support unpacked structs
body:
This change adds support for unpacked structs and for `--structs-packed` flag.

The structs are transformed into regular C++ structs.

The implementation supports embedding dynamic structures inside structs, and assigning struct variables to each other.

A parser-level warning on unpacked array declaration inside a struct has been replaced by a V3Width error on usages of all kinds of compound data inside packed structs -- detection of most of these wasn't implemented, which resulted in generation of invalid C++ code.
 
Worth noting that number assignment to an unpacked struct passes Verilation, but produces C++ that doesn't compile, although I've figured out that this is the case also for dynamic arrays and class objects (see an [example](https://github.com/verilator/verilator/files/10135630/t_assign_const_to_object.zip)). Both probably should be addressed in a separate PR.

`t_struct_unpacked2` test got deleted as it was meant to produce a warning on usage of unsupported unpacked structs. It's replaced by a similar test that produces a warning about unpacked union usage.

The main lacking feature is anonymous aggregate structure assignment (`'{...}`). For that reason [t_structu_dataType_assignment](https://github.com/verilator/verilator/compare/master...antmicro:verilator-1:kiryk/support-unpacked-structs?expand=1#diff-bb7dadf6d3b882bda74bbbafea9719c4f51bea242efc69bcb3a258887be3aa0c) test is weakened to test only packed structs as it in fact did before (with the `--structs-packed` flag).
